TheoremBackedText: UOM/split_controlled_completion_lift_note.tex now theorem-backs a canonical split-to-descendant scalar bridge, a proposition that such a bridge induces canonical scalar datum, and a corollary that the scalar datum and resulting D^- line are canonical once the bridge and c_Lambda != 0 are supplied.
This remains a conditional theorem package: it exposes the bridge as imported transport data rather than deriving it from split-controlled recoherence alone.
The update leaves the separate c_Lambda != 0 lane untouched and does not claim automatic promotion beyond D^- or the current Main-visible quotient.
StillOpenOrImported: Still open/imported: deriving the scalar bridge from branchwise recoherence in B alone.
Still open/imported: the transport-level local intertwiner between the 2T Gamma-odd descendant direction and the compressed UOM-selected odd line.
Still open/imported: canonical identification of the energetic stiffness tilde-alpha(Lambda).
Still separate: c_Lambda != 0 remains an extra input and is not proved by the threshold lane.
